element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player progresses at own pace through organic outdoor environments
Used to purchase power-ups and information at vendor shops

element_introduction_order.element: Enemy defeat and L/E block drops
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Life blocks sustain player, money blocks enable economy participation

element_introduction_order.element: Hidden rooms and Force Star collection
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Required to unlock mechanical castle progression; teaches exploration
Contrasts with outdoor areas; full of robots and machines
Foreshadows even-numbered shooter stages; features door selection puzzle
Pattern-based combat with controllable platform positioning
Full auto-scrolling level with different vehicle each time
Obtained during shooter segments; permanent progression
Obtained during shooter segments; customizable at shops
Provides weapons and items; can be revisited for power-up enhancement

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Accessible via vendors in platforming levels
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Optional side activity for currency generation
Novel rotation mechanic; approaching final boss fortress from sides and bottom
More powerful than previous vehicles; required for final boss

typical_relief_triggers: Completing force star collection (safe exploration reward)
Transition between outdoor and indoor areas
typical_tension_triggers: Auto-scrolling shooter corridor segments with door selection
Boss fights against giant robots with repetitive attack patterns
Transitioning to even-numbered shooter vehicle stages
Final tube shooter approach with rotation mechanics
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating cycle between slow-paced exploration/platforming (odd areas) and fast-paced action/shooting (even areas), with tension building toward mechanical castles and climaxing at boss encounters

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden rooms containing Force Stars; vendor doors and gates scatter shops throughout levels; minigame access indicates optional economic engagement
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden rooms accessed through exploration; goddess character discovered for repeated interactions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Areas 1-2 introduce alternating mechanics gradually: safe platforming with currency/enemy systems, then first shooter vehicle stage
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Areas 3-7 increase mechanical castle complexity and enemy density; shooting patterns become less forgiving; vendor power-ups become essential
difficulty_curve.late_game: Areas 8-9 escalate challenge with refined enemy AI and boss attack patterns; Area 10 introduces novel tube shooter mechanic as final escalation
notable_spikes: First auto-scrolling shooter corridor with door selection (Area 1 exit)
Final tube shooter and Goruza boss encounter (Area 10)

notable_level_patterns.description: Organic outdoor exploration → Hidden Force Star collection → Mechanical castle maze → Auto-scrolling corridor puzzle → Boss fight on floating platform. Emphasizes discovery and environmental contrast.
Vehicle-based Gradius-style auto-scrolling stage with speed/weapon pickups → Boss fight in ship vehicle. Each stage features different vehicle.
Mazelike indoor environment contrasting with organic outdoor areas; full of robots and machines; culminates in auto-scrolling corridor with binary door choice logic.
Giant robot opponents with repetitive, avoidable attack patterns resembling manic shooter design. Black background isolates combat from environment.
Tube shooter (fixed shooter perspective) replacing traditional formulas. Player rotates approach vectors from screen sides to bottom while shooting center. Goruza final boss.
